entities.description: algorithm 1 is the entity resolution process described in the text
v n is a new entity being processed in the entity resolution process
e c represents the top k candidates retrieved for the new entity v n
db is the vector database from which candidates are retrieved
r is the reranker used to re rank candidates against v n
s represents the scores assigned to the candidates
sel is the selection set initialized with the top scoring candidate
g is the gradient threshold used to check score drops
case a occurs when all candidates pass the gradient check  indicating scores lacked discriminative power
case b occurs when a gradient is found  signaling a sharp score drop
v sel is the canonical entity selected from the selection set sel
llm is a tool used to select the canonical entity if multiple aliases are identified
g is a data structure or set that is updated and returned at the end of the process
lines 1 3 describe the initial retrieval and reranking steps of the algorithm
line 4 describes the initialization of the selection set and the initial score
lines 5 8 describe the iteration through remaining candidates and the gradient threshold check
lines 7 8 detail the logic for adding candidates to the selection set and updating scores
line 8 describes the condition where the loop breaks upon detecting a sharp score drop
lines 9 14 describe the final decision making logic of the algorithm
lines 9 10 describe the action taken in case a where a new entity is added
lines 12 14 describe the merging of the new entity with the canonical entity in case b
line 13 describes the use of an llm to select a canonical entity when multiple aliases exist
line 15 describes the return of the updated g and db structures
score is the variable updated during the iteration to track the current score value
v c represents the current candidate being evaluated in the iteration

relations.description: algorithm 1 processes the new entity v n by retrieving candidates and making a decision
algorithm 1 retrieves the top k candidates e c from the vector database db
the vector database db is the source from which candidates e c are retrieved
algorithm 1 uses the reranker r to re rank candidates e c against v n
algorithm 1 sorts candidates based on their scores s
algorithm 1 initializes and iterates through the selection set sel
algorithm 1 uses the gradient threshold g to determine if a score drop is sharp
algorithm 1 identifies case a when the selection set sel is identical to e c
algorithm 1 identifies case b when a gradient is found in the selection set sel
algorithm 1 selects the canonical entity v sel from the selection set sel in case b
algorithm 1 uses an llm to select v sel if multiple aliases are identified
algorithm 1 executes the steps outlined in lines 1 3 to retrieve and rerank candidates
algorithm 1 executes the initialization step described in line 4
algorithm 1 executes the iteration logic described in lines 5 8
the logic in lines 7 8 is part of the iteration process within algorithm 1
line 8 defines the break condition within the loop of algorithm 1
algorithm 1 executes the decision logic described in lines 9 14
lines 9 10 are the specific actions taken when case a is identified in algorithm 1
lines 12 14 are the specific actions taken when case b is identified in algorithm 1
line 13 is a step within the case b logic of algorithm 1
line 15 is the final step of algorithm 1 where results are returned
the variable score is initialized and updated during the execution of algorithm 1
the variable v c is the current candidate processed within the loop of algorithm 1
